text.title: Spring Boot JUnit Testing Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the layered Spring Boot JUnit testing methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every test method MUST be named `methodUnderTest_state_expectedBehaviour` (e.g. `findById_whenUserNotExists_throwsException`). Test class MUST mirror SUT (`UserService` → `UserServiceTest`) in the same package. Test bodies MUST keep `// Arrange / // Act / // Assert` sections visible.
rule.rationale: Self-documenting failure output lets reviewers spot what regression a failure represents without reading the test body.

rule.statement: Controller tests MUST use `@WebMvcTest(SUT.class)` + `@MockBean` service; service tests MUST use `@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)` with no Spring context; repository tests MUST use `@DataJpaTest` + Testcontainers. `@SpringBootTest` MUST appear at most once per module.
rule.rationale: Each unique `@MockBean` set forces Spring to re-create the application context; scattering `@SpringBootTest` multiplies boot cost and makes CI slow + flaky.

rule.statement: Assertions on collections, extracted fields, or non-trivial objects MUST use AssertJ (`assertThat(list).extracting(User::getEmail).containsExactly(...)`). Mixing Hamcrest, AssertJ, and JUnit assertions in the same class is forbidden — pick AssertJ.
rule.rationale: AssertJ's diff identifies the differing element + field; raw JUnit `assertEquals` on lists emits unreadable output.

rule.statement: Async assertions MUST use `Awaitility.await().atMost(...)` polls. `Thread.sleep` is forbidden in test methods. Time-sensitive logic MUST inject a `Clock` bean and assert exact equality.
rule.rationale: `Thread.sleep` is flaky under CI load; Awaitility polls with a configurable interval, failing fast on success.

rule.statement: Repository tests MUST run against the real DB engine via Testcontainers (`PostgreSQLContainer` etc.). `H2`, `HSQLDB`, and `UseInMemoryDatabase` are forbidden for any test that exercises queries, constraints, or migrations.
rule.rationale: H2 dialect diverges from Postgres on JSONB ops, locking, function availability; tests pass on H2 and fail in prod.

rule.statement: CI MUST enforce a Jacoco coverage gate: line ≥ 70 %, branch ≥ 60 %. Mutation testing (Pitest) MUST cover payment, auth, billing paths at least quarterly. Lowering thresholds as a "quick fix" is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Coverage without a gate is advisory only; mutation testing closes the "assertion-free test" loophole.
